Design philosophy: **open and see your data in under three seconds** — your pinned stations front and center, no map to dismiss, no search to type.\n4\t\n5\tVersion 0.0.1\n6\t\n7\t## Preview\n8\t\n9\tScreenshots are available in `docs/`.\n10\t\n11\t## Features\n12\t\n13\t1. **Favorites-first home screen** — pinned stations with inline live arrival countdowns; GPS-powered 60-second onboarding for first-time users\n14\t2. **Real-time arrivals** — live countdowns per station, both directions; pull-to-refresh; 30s auto-refresh; offline fallback via PWA cache\n15\t3. **Transfer intelligence** — commute analyzer shows whether you should transfer using real-time train positions: RECOMMENDED / DIRECT / ALSO POSSIBLE, with walk-time comparison\n16\t4. **Predictive delay detection** — tracks vehicle positions across consecutive 30s polls; when a train's inter-station time exceeds 2× the scheduled baseline, generates early-warning synthetic alerts before MTA publishes them\n17\t5. **Smart alerts** — filtered to your exact stations, lines, and directions; plain-language rewrites of raw MTA alert text; push notifications via WebPush/VAPID\n18\t6. **Context-aware switching** — detects home/commute/transfer context from location, time patterns, and tap history; frequency-sorts favorites during commute hours\n19\t7. **Interactive transit map** — SVG map with pan/zoom, real-time pulsing train positions, line filtering, and tap-to-detail modal\n20\t8. **Trip journal** — automatic trip tracking with full history and statistics (average, median, std dev, trends)\n21\t9. **Subway Year** — shareable annual summary card (Spotify Wrapped-style), exportable as PNG; configurable time window\n22\t10. **Fare tracking** — OMNY fare cap progress and weekly spending\n23\t11. **Elevator/escalator status** — equipment status per station from the MTA ENE feed\n24\t12. **Health dashboard** — `/status` HTML page and `/api/health` JSON endpoint with per-feed circuit-breaker state\n25\t\n26\t## Transit Data\n27\t\n28\tAll real-time data is from the [MTA GTFS-RT Protobuf API](https://api.mta.info/). An API key is optional but recommended to avoid rate limiting — register free at https://api.mta.info/\n29\t\n30\t| Feed | Lines |\n31\t|------|-------|\n32\t| gtfs | 1 2 3 4 5 6 7 S GS |\n33\t| gtfs-ace | A C E H FS |\n34\t| gtfs-bdfm | B D F M |\n35\t| gtfs-g | G |\n36\t| gtfs-jz | J Z |\n37\t| gtfs-l | L |\n38\t| gtfs-nqrw | N Q R W |\n39\t| gtfs-si | SIR (Staten Island Railway) |\n40\t\n41\tPolling intervals: 30s (arrivals), 60s (alerts), 300s (equipment). Static schedule from MTA's published GTFS ZIP.\n42\t\n43\t## Quick Start\n44\t\n45\t**Prerequisites:** Node.js ≥ 22, npm ≥ 10\n46\t\n47\t```bash\n48\tgit clone https://github.com/jedarden/mta-my-way.git\n49\tcd mta-my-way\n50\tnpm install\n51\t\n52\t# One-time: process GTFS static schedule data\n53\tnpm run process-gtfs --workspace=packages/server\n54\t\n55\t# Start development server\n56\tnpm run dev --workspace=packages/server\n57\t```\n58\t\n59\tApp available at `http://localhost:3001`. Set `MTA_API_KEY` in your environment to avoid MTA rate limits during development.\n60\t\n61\t## Docker\n62\t\n63\t```bash\n64\tdocker build -t mta-my-way .\n65\tdocker run \\\n66\t  -e ALLOWED_HOSTS=your.domain.com \\\n67\t  -e MTA_API_KEY=your_key_here \\\n68\t  -p 3000:3000 \\\n69\t  mta-my-way\n70\t```\n71\t\n72\tThe container exposes port 3000.\n73\t\n74\t## Environment Variables\n75\t\n76\t| Variable | Required | Default | Notes |\n77\t|----------|----------|---------|-------|\n78\t| `PORT` | No | `3000` | HTTP listen port |\n79\t| `NODE_ENV` | No | — | `production` or `development` |\n80\t| `ALLOWED_HOSTS` | Yes (prod) | — | Comma-separated hostnames; server refuses to start without this in production |\n81\t| `MTA_API_KEY` | Recommended | — | MTA GTFS-RT API key; works without but may be rate-limited. Register at https://api.mta.info/ |\n82\t| `VAPID_PUBLIC_KEY` | For push | — | Generate: `npx web-push generate-vapid-keys` |\n83\t| `VAPID_PRIVATE_KEY` | For push | — | Never commit to version control |\n84\t| `VAPID_SUBJECT` | For push | `mailto:mta-my-way@example.com` | Contact URI included in push requests |\n85\t| `PASSWORD_PEPPER` | Recommended | — | `openssl rand -hex 32` |\n86\t| `EMAIL_PROVIDER` | No | `console` | `ses`, `smtp`, `sendgrid`, or `console` |\n87\t| `EMAIL_FROM` | No | `noreply@mtamyway.com` | Sender address for transactional email |\n88\t| `LOG_LEVEL` | No | `info` | `debug`, `info`, `warn`, `error` |\n89\t\n90\t## Development Commands\n91\t\n92\t```bash\n93\tnpm test                        # all unit/integration tests (Vitest)\n94\tnpm run test:watch              # watch mode\n95\tcd tests/e2e && npm test        # Playwright E2E tests\n96\tnpm run lint                    # Biome + ESLint\n97\tnpm run format                  # Biome format --write\n98\tnpm run typecheck               # tsc --build\n99\t```\n100\t\n101\t**Code style:** Biome for formatting, TypeScript strict mode throughout. Commit prefix convention: `feat:`, `fix:`, `docs:`, `refactor:`, `test:`.\n102\t\n103\t## API Reference\n104\t\n105\t| Method | Path | Description |\n106\t|--------|------|-------------|\n107\t| GET | `/status` | Health dashboard (HTML) |\n108\t| GET | `/api/health` | Per-feed status and circuit-breaker state (JSON) |\n109\t| GET | `/api/metrics` | Prometheus metrics |\n110\t| GET | `/api/arrivals/:stationId` | Real-time arrivals for a station |\n111\t| GET | `/api/stations` | Full GTFS station list |\n112\t| GET | `/api/stations/search` | Type-ahead search by name, line, or cross-street |\n113\t| GET | `/api/routes` | Route index |\n114\t| POST | `/api/commute/analyze` | Analyze routes between origin and destination |\n115\t| GET | `/api/alerts` | All current MTA alerts |\n116\t| GET | `/api/alerts/:lineId` | Alerts filtered by line |\n117\t| GET | `/api/push/vapid-public-key` | VAPID public key for push registration |\n118\t| POST | `/api/push/subscribe` | Register device for push notifications |\n119\t| GET | `/api/trip/:tripId` | Live trip progress (stop-by-stop) |\n120\t\n121\t## Observability\n122\t\n123\tOpenTelemetry (OTLP gRPC), Prometheus `/api/metrics`, Pino structured JSON logging. See [docs/observability.md](docs/observability.md) for configuration details.\n124\t\n125\t## Project Structure\n126\t\n127\t```\n128\tmta-my-way/\n129\t├── packages/\n130\t│   ├── shared/           # TypeScript types and constants (feed config, polling intervals)\n131\t│   ├── server/\n132\t│   │   ├── src/          # Hono app, pollers, GTFS parsers, push engine, context engine\n133\t│   │   ├── data/         # Generated GTFS JSON + SQLite (subscriptions, trips, sessions)\n134\t│   │   └── scripts/      # process-gtfs.mjs\n135\t│   └── web/\n136\t│       └── src/\n137\t│           ├── screens/  # Home, Station, Map, Commute, Alerts, Journal, Stats, ...\n138\t│           ├── components/\n139\t│           ├── hooks/\n140\t│           └── stores/   # Zustand (favorites, journal, settings, fare)\n141\t├── tests/\n142\t│   └── e2e/              # Playwright tests\n143\t├── docs/\n144\t│   ├── plan/plan.md      # Architecture and roadmap\n145\t│   ├── testing.md\n146\t│   ├── security.md\n147\t│   └── observability.md\n148\t└── Dockerfile            # Multi-stage: build-web → build-server → runtime\n149\t```\n150\t\n151\t## Documentation\n152\t\n153\t- [Testing Guide](docs/testing.md)\n154\t- [Security](docs/security.md)\n155\t- [Observability](docs/observability.md)\n156\t- [Architecture Plan](docs/plan/plan.md)\n157\t\n158\t## License\n159\t\n160\tMIT\n161\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"8dc110af-4787-4b15-a2f3-16a5301690b0","uuid":"eca59709-4471-4684-b6b3-2cab51c9ebf9","timestamp":"2026-07-03T19:14:44.146Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/home/coding/mta-my-way/README.md","content":"# MTA My Way\n\nA mobile-first Progressive Web App (PWA) for daily NYC subway commuters. Design philosophy: **open and see your data in under three seconds** — your pinned stations front and center, no map to dismiss, no search to type.\n\nVersion 0.0.1\n\n## Preview\n\nScreenshots are available in `docs/`.\n\n## Features\n\n1. **Favorites-first home screen** — pinned stations with inline live arrival countdowns; GPS-powered 60-second onboarding for first-time users\n2. **Real-time arrivals** — live countdowns per station, both directions; pull-to-refresh; 30s auto-refresh; offline fallback via PWA cache\n3. **Transfer intelligence** — commute analyzer shows whether you should transfer using real-time train positions: RECOMMENDED / DIRECT / ALSO POSSIBLE, with walk-time comparison\n4. **Predictive delay detection** — tracks vehicle positions across consecutive 30s polls; when a train's inter-station time exceeds 2× the scheduled baseline, generates early-warning synthetic alerts before MTA publishes them\n5. **Smart alerts** — filtered to your exact stations, lines, and directions; plain-language rewrites of raw MTA alert text; push notifications via WebPush/VAPID\n6. **Context-aware switching** — detects home/commute/transfer context from location, time patterns, and tap history; frequency-sorts favorites during commute hours\n7. **Interactive transit map** — SVG map with pan/zoom, real-time pulsing train positions, line filtering, and tap-to-detail modal\n8. **Trip journal** — automatic trip tracking with full history and statistics (average, median, std dev, trends)\n9. **Subway Year** — shareable annual summary card (Spotify Wrapped-style), exportable as PNG; configurable time window\n10. **Fare tracking** — OMNY fare cap progress and weekly spending\n11. **Elevator/escalator status** — equipment status per station from the MTA ENE feed\n12. **Health dashboard** — `/status` HTML page and `/api/health` JSON endpoint with per-feed circuit-breaker state\n\n## Transit Data\n\nAll real-time data is from the [MTA GTFS-RT Protobuf API](https://api.mta.info/). An API key is optional but recommended to avoid rate limiting — register free at https://api.mta.info/\n\n| Feed | Lines |\n|------|-------|\n| gtfs | 1 2 3 4 5 6 7 S GS |\n| gtfs-ace | A C E H FS |\n| gtfs-bdfm | B D F M |\n| gtfs-g | G |\n| gtfs-jz | J Z |\n| gtfs-l | L |\n| gtfs-nqrw | N Q R W |\n| gtfs-si | SIR (Staten Island Railway) |\n\nPolling intervals: 30s (arrivals), 60s (alerts), 300s (equipment). Static schedule from MTA's published GTFS ZIP.\n\n## Quick Start\n\n**Prerequisites:** Node.js ≥ 22, npm ≥ 10\n\n```bash\ngit clone https://github.com/jedarden/mta-my-way.git\ncd mta-my-way\nnpm install\n\n# One-time: process GTFS static schedule data\nnpm run process-gtfs --workspace=packages/server\n\n# Start development server\nnpm run dev --workspace=packages/server\n```\n\nApp available at `http://localhost:3001`. Set `MTA_API_KEY` in your environment to avoid MTA rate limits during development.\n\n## Docker\n\n```bash\ndocker build -t mta-my-way .\ndocker run \\\n  -e ALLOWED_HOSTS=your.domain.com \\\n  -e MTA_API_KEY=your_key_here \\\n  -p 3000:3000 \\\n  mta-my-way\n```\n\nThe container exposes port 3000.\n\n## Environment Variables\n\n| Variable | Required | Default | Notes |\n|----------|----------|---------|-------|\n| `PORT` | No | `3000` | HTTP listen port |\n| `NODE_ENV` | No | — | `production` or `development` |\n| `ALLOWED_HOSTS` | Yes (prod) | — | Comma-separated hostnames; server refuses to start without this in production |\n| `MTA_API_KEY` | Recommended | — | MTA GTFS-RT API key; works without but may be rate-limited. Register at https://api.mta.info/ |\n| `VAPID_PUBLIC_KEY` | For push | — | Generate: `npx web-push generate-vapid-keys` |\n| `VAPID_PRIVATE_KEY` | For push | — | Never commit to version control |\n| `VAPID_SUBJECT` | For push | `mailto:mta-my-way@example.com` | Contact URI included in push requests |\n| `PASSWORD_PEPPER` | Recommended | — | `openssl rand -hex 32` |\n| `EMAIL_PROVIDER` | No | `console` | `ses`, `smtp`, `sendgrid`, or `console` |\n| `EMAIL_FROM` | No | `noreply@mtamyway.com` | Sender address for transactional email |\n| `LOG_LEVEL` | No | `info` | `debug`, `info`, `warn`, `error` |\n\n## Development Commands\n\n```bash\nnpm test                        # all unit/integration tests (Vitest)\nnpm run test:watch              # watch mode\ncd tests/e2e && npm test        # Playwright E2E tests\nnpm run lint                    # Biome + ESLint\nnpm run format                  # Biome format --write\nnpm run typecheck               # tsc --build\n```\n\n**Code style:** Biome for formatting, TypeScript strict mode throughout. Commit prefix convention: `feat:`, `fix:`, `docs:`, `refactor:`, `test:`.\n\n## API Reference\n\n| Method | Path | Description |\n|--------|------|-------------|\n| GET | `/status` | Health dashboard (HTML) |\n| GET | `/api/health` | Per-feed status and circuit-breaker state (JSON) |\n| GET | `/api/metrics` | Prometheus metrics |\n| GET | `/api/arrivals/:stationId` | Real-time arrivals for a station |\n| GET | `/api/stations` | Full GTFS station list |\n| GET | `/api/stations/search` | Type-ahead search by name, line, or cross-street |\n| GET | `/api/routes` | Route index |\n| POST | `/api/commute/analyze` | Analyze routes between origin and destination |\n| GET | `/api/alerts` | All current MTA alerts |\n| GET | `/api/alerts/:lineId` | Alerts filtered by line |\n| GET | `/api/push/vapid-public-key` | VAPID public key for push registration |\n| POST | `/api/push/subscribe` | Register device for push notifications |\n| GET | `/api/trip/:tripId` | Live trip progress (stop-by-stop) |\n\n## Observability\n\nOpenTelemetry (OTLP gRPC), Prometheus `/api/metrics`, Pino structured JSON logging. See [docs/observability.md](docs/observability.md) for configuration details.\n\n## Project Structure\n\n```\nmta-my-way/\n├── packages/\n│   ├── shared/           # TypeScript types and constants (feed config, polling intervals)\n│   ├── server/\n│   │   ├── src/          # Hono app, pollers, GTFS parsers, push engine, context engine\n│   │   ├── data/         # Generated GTFS JSON + SQLite (subscriptions, trips, sessions)\n│   │   └── scripts/      # process-gtfs.mjs\n│   └── web/\n│       └── src/\n│           ├── screens/  # Home, Station, Map, Commute, Alerts, Journal, Stats, ...\n│           ├── components/\n│           ├── hooks/\n│           └── stores/   # Zustand (favorites, journal, settings, fare)\n├── tests/\n│   └── e2e/              # Playwright tests\n├── docs/\n│   ├── plan/plan.md      # Architecture and roadmap\n│   ├── testing.md\n│   ├── security.md\n│   └── observability.md\n└── Dockerfile            # Multi-stage: build-web → build-server → runtime\n```\n\n## Documentation\n\n- [Testing Guide](docs/testing.md)\n- [Security](docs/security.md)\n- [Observability](docs/observability.md)\n- [Architecture Plan](docs/plan/plan.md)\n\n## License\n\nMIT\n","numLines":161,"startLine":1,"totalLines":161}}}

measures.\\n\\n## Defense in Depth\\n\\nSecurity is implemented in layers:\\n\\n1. **Cloudflare WAF** (first line of defense)\\n2. **Hono middleware** (second line of defense)\\n3. **Zod input validation** (third line of defense)\\n\\n## Cloudflare WAF Configuration\\n\\nConfigure in the Cloudflare tunnel dashboard:\\n\\n- **Rate-limit rule**: 100 requests/minute per IP for `/api/*` paths\\n- Returns 429 with a `Retry-After` header\\n- Cloudflare Bot Management (free tier) filters automated abuse\\n\\n## Hono Rate Limiting\\n\\n**File**: `packages/server/src/middleware/rate-limiter.ts`\\n\\nIn-memory token bucket rate limiter:\\n- **Limit**: 60 requests/minute per IP\\n- **Algorithm**: Token bucket (1 token/second refill)\\n- **Client identification**: Uses `CF-Connecting-IP` header from Cloudflare tunnel, falls back to `X-Forwarded-For`\\n- **Memory management**: Periodic pruning of stale buckets every 5 minutes\\n\\n```typescript\\n// Example 429 response\\n{\\n  \\\"error\\\": \\\"Too many requests\\\",\\n  \\\"retryAfter\\\": 42\\n}\\n```\\n\\n**Note**: In-memory rate limiter resets on pod restart — acceptable for single-container deployment. Cloudflare WAF is the first line of defense.\\n\\n## Zod Input Validation\\n\\n**Files**:\\n- `packages/shared/src/schemas/push.ts` — Push notification schemas\\n- `packages/shared/src/schemas/commute.ts` — Commute analysis schemas\\n- `packages/server/src/middleware/validation.ts` — Validation helper\\n\\nAll API inputs are validated against Zod schemas before processing. Invalid payloads return 400 with structured error details:\\n\\n```typescript\\n// Example 400 response\\n{\\n  \\\"error\\\": \\\"Validation failed\\\",\\n  \\\"details\\\": [\\n    { \\\"field\\\": \\\"subscription.endpoint\\\", \\\"message\\\": \\\"Must be a valid URL\\\" },\\n    { \\\"field\\\": \\\"favorites[0].lines\\\", \\\"message\\\": \\\"Array must contain at least 1 item(s)\\\" }\\n  ]\\n}\\n```\\n\\n### Push Subscription Schemas\\n\\n- `pushSubscribeRequestSchema` — POST /api/push/subscribe\\n- `pushUnsubscribeRequestSchema` — DELETE /api/push/unsubscribe\\n- `pushUpdateRequestSchema` — PATCH /api/push/subscription\\n\\n### Commute Analysis Schema\\n\\n- `commuteAnalyzeRequestSchema` — POST /api/commute/analyze\\n\\n## Security Headers\\n\\n**File**: `packages/server/src/middleware/security-headers.ts`\\n\\nAll responses include:\\n\\n| Header | Value | Purpose |\\n|--------|-------|---------|\\n| `Content-Security-Policy` | `default-src 'self'; script-src 'self'; style-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline'; img-src 'self' data:; connect-src 'self'; font-src 'self'; manifest-src 'self'; worker-src 'self'` | Prevents XSS attacks |\\n| `X-Content-Type-Options` | `nosniff` | Prevents MIME type sniffing |\\n| `X-Frame-Options` | `DENY` | Prevents clickjacking |\\n| `Referrer-Policy` | `strict-origin-when-cross-origin` | Controls referrer information |\\n| `Strict-Transport-Security` | `max-age=31536000; includeSubDomains` | HSTS for 1 year |\\n\\n### CSP Notes\\n\\n- `'unsafe-inline'` for styles is required for Tailwind CSS utility classes\\n- No `'unsafe-inline'` or `'unsafe-eval'` for scripts\\n- `data:` allowed for images (inline icons)\\n\\n## Trip Share Link TTL\\n\\n**File**: `packages/server/src/trip-lookup.ts`\\n\\nTrip share links (`/trip/:tripId`) have a 24-hour TTL enforced server-side:\\n\\n- First access records the timestamp\\n- Subsequent accesses check if 24 hours have elapsed\\n- Expired trips return 404\\n- Memory cleanup prunes stale entries every 5 minutes\\n\\n```typescript\\nconst TRIP_SHARE_TTL_MS = 24 * 60 * 60 * 1000; // 24 hours\\n```\\n\\n## Data Privacy (No PII Server-Side)\\n\\n**File**: `packages/server/src/push/subscriptions.ts`\\n\\nPush subscriptions are stored with privacy-by-design:\\n\\n- **Keyed by SHA-256 hash** of the subscription endpoint URL\\n- **No user identity** stored (no email, device ID, or account)\\n- **No location data** — geolocation is client-side only, never sent to backend\\n- **No PII in favorites** — favorites/commutes stored in browser localStorage, never sent to server\\n\\n```typescript\\nfunction hashEndpoint(endpoint: string): string {\\n  return createHash(\\\"sha256\\\").update(endpoint).digest(\\\"hex\\\");\\n}\\n```\\n\\n### Data Stored Server-Side\\n\\n| Data | Storage | PII? |\\n|------|---------|------|\\n| Push subscription keys | SQLite | No (hashed endpoint) |\\n| Favorite tuples (station/line/direction) | SQLite | No (transit data only) |\\n| Quiet hours settings | SQLite | No (preference only) |\\n| Morning scores | SQLite | No (preference only) |\\n\\n### Data Stored Client-Side Only\\n\\n- Full favorites list with labels\\n- Commute configurations\\n- Trip journal/history\\n- Fare tracking (OMNY cap)\\n- Tap history for context sorting\\n- Settings (theme, refresh interval, etc.)\\n- Geolocation coordinates\\n\\n## Security Checklist\\n\\n- [x] Cloudflare WAF: rate-limit rule for /api/* at 100 req/min per IP\\n- [x] Hono middleware: in-memory token bucket rate limiter (60 req/min per IP)\\n- [x] Zod schemas for all API inputs: push subscription payload, commute analysis request\\n- [x] Return 400 with structured error for invalid payloads\\n- [x] CSP headers: default-src 'self', script-src 'self', style-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline'\\n- [x] Security headers: X-Content-Type-Options, X-Frame-Options, Referrer-Policy\\n- [x] Trip share link TTL: 24h expiry enforced server-side\\n- [x] Audit: verify no PII stored server-side (push subs keyed by hash, no user identity)\\n\",\"structuredPatch\":[{\"oldStart\":139,\"oldLines\":6,\"newStart\":139,\"newLines\":26,\"lines\":[\" - Settings (theme, refresh interval, etc.)\",\" - Geolocation coordinates\",\" validation** (third line of defense)\\n\\n## Cloudflare WAF Configuration\\n\\nConfigure in the Cloudflare tunnel dashboard:\\n\\n- **Rate-limit rule**: 100 requests/minute per IP for `/api/*` paths\\n- Returns 429 with a `Retry-After` header\\n- Cloudflare Bot Management (free tier) filters automated abuse\\n\\n## Hono Rate Limiting\\n\\n**File**: `packages/server/src/middleware/rate-limiter.ts`\\n\\nIn-memory token bucket rate limiter:\\n- **Limit**: 60 requests/minute per IP\\n- **Algorithm**: Token bucket (1 token/second refill)\\n- **Client identification**: Uses `CF-Connecting-IP` header from Cloudflare tunnel, falls back to `X-Forwarded-For`\\n- **Memory management**: Periodic pruning of stale buckets every 5 minutes\\n\\n```typescript\\n// Example 429 response\\n{\\n  \\\"error\\\": \\\"Too many requests\\\",\\n  \\\"retryAfter\\\": 42\\n}\\n```\\n\\n**Note**: In-memory rate limiter resets on pod restart ]\\n}\\n```\\n\\n### Push Subscription Schemas\\n\\n- `pushSubscribeRequestSchema` — POST /api/push/subscribe\\n- `pushUnsubscribeRequestSchema` — DELETE /api/push/unsubscribe\\n- `pushUpdateRequestSchema` — PATCH /api/push/subscription\\n\\n### Commute Analysis Schema\\n\\n- `commuteAnalyzeRequestSchema` — POST /api/commute/analyze\\n\\n## Security Headers\\n\\n**File**: `packages/server/src/middleware/security-headers.ts`\\n\\nAll responses include:\\n\\n| Header | Value | Purpose |\\n|--------|-------|---------|\\n| `Content-Security-Policy` | `default-src 'self'; script-src 'self'; style-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline'; img-src 'self' data:; connect-src 'self'; font-src 'self'; manifest-src 'self'; worker-src 'self'` | Prevents XSS attacks |\\n| `X-Content-Type-Options` | `nosniff` | Prevents MIME type sniffing |\\n| `X-Frame-Options` | `DENY` | Prevents clickjacking |\\n| `Referrer-Policy` | `strict-origin-when-cross-origin` | Controls referrer information |\\n| `Strict-Transport-Security` | `max-age=31536000; includeSubDomains` | HSTS for 1 year |\\n\\n### CSP Notes\\n\\n- `'unsafe-inline'` for styles is required for Tailwind CSS utility classes\\n- No `'unsafe-inline'` or `'unsafe-eval'` for scripts\\n- `data:` allowed for images (inline icons)\\n\\n## Trip Share Link TTL\\n\\n**File**: `packages/server/src/trip-lookup.ts`\\n\\nTrip share links (`/trip/:tripId`) have a 24-hour TTL enforced server-side:\\n\\n- First access records the timestamp\\n- Subsequent accesses check if 24 hours have elapsed\\n- Expired trips return 404\\n- Memory cleanup prunes stale entries every 5 minutes\\n\\n```typescript\\nconst TRIP_SHARE_TTL_MS = 24 * 60 * 60 * 1000; // 24 hours\\n```\\n\\n## Data Privacy (No PII Server-Side)\\n\\n**File**: `packages/server/src/push/subscriptions.ts`\\n\\nPush subscriptions are stored with privacy-by-design:\\n\\n- **Keyed by SHA-256 hash** of the subscription endpoint URL\\n- **No user identity** stored (no email, device ID, or account)\\n- **No location data** — geolocation is client-side only, never sent to backend\\n- **No PII in favorites** — favorites/commutes stored in browser localStorage, never sent to server\\n\\n```typescript\\nfunction hashEndpoint(endpoint: string): string {\\n  return createHash(\\\"sha256\\\").update(endpoint).digest(\\\"hex\\\");\\n}\\n```\\n\\n### Data Stored Server-Side\\n\\n| Data | Storage | PII? |\\n|------|---------|------|\\n| Push subscription keys | SQLite | No (hashed endpoint) |\\n| Favorite tuples (station/line/direction) | SQLite | No (transit data only) |\\n| Quiet hours settings | SQLite | No (preference only) |\\n| Morning scores | SQLite | No (preference only) |\\n\\n### Data Stored Client-Side Only\\n\\n- Full favorites list with labels\\n- Commute configurations\\n- Trip journal/history\\n- Fare tracking (OMNY cap)\\n- Tap history It uses **Protocol Buffers (protobuf)** -- a compact binary serialization format -- for efficient data transmission.\n82\t\n83\tThe MTA's implementation extends the base GTFS-RT specification with **NYCT-specific extensions** that provide additional subway-specific data.\n84\t\n85\t### 3.2 Feed Endpoints\n86\t\n87\t#### Subway Feeds (by line group)\n88\t\n89\tAll subway feeds use the base URL: `https://api-endpoint.mta.info/Dataservice/mtagtfsfeeds/nyct/`\n90\t\n91\t| Feed | Lines Covered | Endpoint |\n92\t|------|--------------|----------|\n93\t| **1-7, S (A Division)** | 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, S (42nd St Shuttle) | `.../nyct/gtfs` |\n94\t| **ACE** | A, C, E, H (Rockaway Shuttle), FS (Franklin Shuttle) | `.../nyct/gtfs-ace` |\n95\t| **BDFM** | B, D, F, M | `.../nyct/gtfs-bdfm` |\n96\t| **G** | G | `.../nyct/gtfs-g` |\n97\t| **JZ** | J, Z | `.../nyct/gtfs-jz` |\n98\t| **L** | L | `.../nyct/gtfs-l` |\n99\t| **NQRW** | N, Q, R, W | `.../nyct/gtfs-nqrw` |\n100\t| **SIR** | Staten Island Railway | `.../nyct/gtfs-si` |"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"8dc110af-4787-4b15-a2f3-16a5301690b0","uuid":"1eed65ef-0c39-4f4a-8c8d-622530da031c","timestamp":"2026-07-03T19:15:44.995Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/home/coding/mta-my-way/docs/research/mta-api-research.md","content":"# MTA Real-Time Train Arrival API Research\n\n> Research conducted: 2026-03-19\n\n---\n\n## Table of Contents\n\n1. [MTA API Overview](#1-mta-api-overview)\n2. [Authentication and Access](#2-authentication-and-access)\n3. [GTFS-RT (General Transit Feed Specification - Realtime)](#3-gtfs-rt-general-transit-feed-specification---realtime)\n4. [GTFS Static Data](#4-gtfs-static-data)\n5. [How to Get Train Arrival Times at a Specific Station](#5-how-to-get-train-arrival-times-at-a-specific-station)\n6. [Alternative and Supplementary APIs](#6-alternative-and-supplementary-apis)\n7. [Common Libraries and Tools](#7-common-libraries-and-tools)\n8. [Known Limitations and Gotchas](#8-known-limitations-and-gotchas)\n\n---\n\n## 1. MTA API Overview\n\nThe Metropolitan Transportation Authority (MTA) provides several data APIs for developers to access real-time and static transit information across its services: NYC Subway, Long Island Rail Road (LIRR), Metro-North Railroad (MNR), and MTA Bus.\n\n### Available APIs\n\n| API | Format | Coverage | Use Case |\n|-----|--------|----------|----------|\n| **GTFS-RT (Realtime)** | Protocol Buffers | Subway, LIRR, MNR | Real-time vehicle positions, trip updates, arrival predictions |\n| **GTFS Static** | CSV (zipped) | Subway, LIRR, MNR, Bus | Schedules, stop/station data, route definitions |\n| **GTFS Alerts** | Protocol Buffers | All modes | Service alerts, delays, planned work |\n| **SIRI (Bus Time)** | XML / JSON | MTA Bus, NYCT Bus | Real-time bus positions, stop monitoring |\n| **OneBusAway REST** | JSON | Bus | Discovery services, stop/route lookup |\n\n### Primary Developer Resources\n\n- **Developer portal**: https://www.mta.info/developers\n- **API gateway**: https://api.mta.info\n- **Legacy data mine (may be deprecated)**: https://datamine.mta.info\n- **Developer support (Google Group)**: https://groups.google.com/g/mtadeveloperresources\n\n---\n\n## 2. Authentication and Access\n\n### Current Status: No API Key Required for Subway GTFS-RT\n\nAs of the most recent updates, **API keys are no longer required** to access MTA GTFS-RT subway feeds. The feeds at `api-endpoint.mta.info` can be fetched directly without authentication headers.\n\nThis was confirmed by the `nyct-gtfs` library (v2.0.0+) and the `underground` library, both of which removed API key requirements.\n\n### Historical Context\n\nPreviously, developers needed to:\n1. Register at https://api.mta.info/#/landing\n2. Agree to the MTA Data Feed Agreement\n3. Obtain an API key\n4. Pass the key in requests (via header or query parameter)\n\n### Bus Time API (Still Requires Key)\n\nThe SIRI-based Bus Time API **still requires** an API key:\n- Register at: http://bt.mta.info/wiki/Developers/Index\n- Rate limit: **1 request per 30 seconds** (strictly enforced)\n\n### LIRR and Metro-North\n\nThese feeds may still require API key authentication. Contact MTA developer support for access details.\n\n### Terms of Use\n\n- Free for public-facing, non-commercial applications\n- Commercial use requires a separate licensing agreement\n- Full terms: https://api.mta.info/#/DataFeedAgreement\n\n---\n\n## 3. GTFS-RT (General Transit Feed Specification - Realtime)\n\n### 3.1 Overview\n\nGTFS-RT is a standardized format (maintained by MobilityData, originally created by Google) for transmitting real-time transit information. It uses **Protocol Buffers (protobuf)** -- a compact binary serialization format -- for efficient data transmission.\n\nThe MTA's implementation extends the base GTFS-RT specification with **NYCT-specific extensions** that provide additional subway-specific data.\n\n### 3.2 Feed Endpoints\n\n#### Subway Feeds (by line group)\n\nAll subway feeds use the base URL: `https://api-endpoint.mta.info/Dataservice/mtagtfsfeeds/nyct/`\n\n| Feed | Lines Covered | Endpoint |\n|------|--------------|----------|\n| **1-7, S (A Division)** | 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, S (42nd St Shuttle) | `.../nyct/gtfs` |\n| **ACE** | A, C, E, H (Rockaway Shuttle), FS (Franklin Shuttle) | `.../nyct/gtfs-ace` |\n| **BDFM** | B, D, F, M | `.../nyct/gtfs-bdfm` |\n| **G** | G | `.../nyct/gtfs-g` |\n| **JZ** | J, Z | `.../nyct/gtfs-jz` |\n| **L** | L | `.../nyct/gtfs-l` |\n| **NQRW** | N, Q, R, W | `.../nyct/gtfs-nqrw` |\n| **SIR** | Staten Island Railway | `.../nyct/gtfs-si` |","numLines":100,"startLine":1,"totalLines":904}}}
